title: Meat Industry (Inspection of Meat Industry Stakeholder’s Activities) Regulations, 2019
title_en: Meat Industry (Inspection of Meat Industry Stakeholder’s Activities) Regulations, 2019

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Meat Industry (Inspection of Meat Industry Stakeholder’s Activities) Regulations, 2019 and shall come into operation on such date as the Minister may, by notice published in the Gazette appoint.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to all stakeholders’ activities along meat value chain in Mainland Tanzania.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires: “ Act ” Means the Meat Industry Act; “ electric prods and sticks ” means a device used to make cattle or other livestock move by striking; “ establishment ” means a place designated for the purpose of keeping, trading, slaughtering of livestock, processing or selling meat and meat products; “ grazing land ” means a developed or undeveloped land used for grazing; “ SSOPs ” stands for Standard Sanitary Operating Procedures; “ HACCP ” stands for Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point; “ methane emissions ” means gas from enteric fermentation produced from decaying organic wastes of solid waste landfills. The gas forms a major component of natural gas and has high global warming potential; “ Livestock Production Units ” means a ranch or communal grazing land or livestock farm with established boundaries and relevant infrastructure used for the production of all kinds of livestock; “ livestock market ” means establishment where livestock are traded by auction; “ Inspector ” means a person appointed by the Minister amongst livestock professionals in public service for inspecting stakeholders' activity, establishments and relevant documents in meat business with the compliance of the Act . [Cap. 421] Part II – Appointment, duties and powers of Inspectors

provisions.title: Appointment of Inspectors
provisions.content: (1) The Minister may, by notice published in the Gazette , and upon recommendation by the Board, appoint such number of staff to be Inspectors.

provisions.title: Identification cards
provisions.content: (1) The Registrar shall issue identification card for each appointed Inspector . (2) The identification card issued under sub regulation (1) , shall be in the form set out in the First Schedule.

provisions.title: Tenure of office of Inspector
provisions.content: (1) An Inspector gazetted pursuant to these Regulations shall hold an office for a term of five years and may be eligible for re-appointment. (2) The appointment of an Inspector shall be terminated upon the occurrence of any of the following: (a) death; (b) resignation or retirement; (c) termination of appointment; or (d) any circumstances that prohibit or impede the Inspector from performing his functions.

provisions.title: Functions of inspector
provisions.content: (1) An Inspector , shall perform the following functions: (a) to inspect establishment and stakeholders’ activities within their respective area of operation; (b) to examine or inspect quality of livestock husbandry procedures, livestock marketing procedure, meat and meat products production process, meat business, certificate of registration, license, record book, electronic information storage or any other document in the establishment ; and for that purpose, and doing other things including taking of extracts from documents, as may be necessary to effect the examination of proper inspection; (c) to inspect the compliance of relevant requirement for abattoir, slaughter house, slab, as provided in the Sixth Schedule of these Regulations; (d) to inspect the compliance of relevant requirement for nyama choma or butcher as provided in the Seventh Schedule of these Regulations; and (e) any other function as may be directed by the Registrar. (2) An Inspector under these Regulations shall prepare and submit a report of his findings to the Registrar immediately after effecting inspection. (3) The report prepared under sub regulation (2) shall be in the form as prescribed in the Second Schedule. (4) The Registrar shall consider the report submitted under sub regulation (2) and take action if any and submit to the Board.

provisions.title: Powers of Inspector
provisions.content: (1) An Inspector in the performance of his functions under Regulation 7 shall have the power to— (a) enter any establishment for the purpose of inspection and to require the owner of the establishment to give access to livestock, meat or meat products for that purpose to inspect its conditions or maintenance of established quality standards of the products; (b) issue prohibition notice to owners or operators of establishment who operate in contravention of prescribed procedures; (c) close an establishment found to contravene the Act and any other written law in force; (d) inquire into any matter regarding livestock production or livestock meat, or meat products marketing within their areas; (e) seize and detain any equipment, record or any other thing which appear to him to constitute or contain evidence or a contravention of any provision of these Regulations and fill in seizure form as described in the Third Schedule; (f) inspect livestock production unit, livestock market , slaughter facility, butcher, nyama choma, and any place where activities involving livestock and meat are operated.

provisions.title: Termination of services of Inspector
provisions.content: (1) The Minister may upon recommendation of the Registrar terminate the service of an Inspector where he fails without good cause— (a) to perform his duties; (b) to observe any of the condition attached on his identification card; and (c) to abide to the rules relating to ethics. (2) An Inspector who has been terminated or resigned or retires from his office shall within seven days from the date of his termination, submit the identification card issued to him to the Registrar of the Board. (3) An Inspector , who fails to submit an identification card within the period specified under sub regulation (2) , commits an offence. Part III – Inspection of establishments

provisions.title: Inspection of stakeholders
provisions.content: Every Inspection of stakeholders’ activities, establishment or documents shall be performed in the presence of— (a) a person in authority at stakeholder’s activity, establishment or documents; (b) a stakeholder; (c) the agent of the stakeholder or establishment ; or (d) the person in possession of the establishment .

provisions.title: Husbandry inspection requirements
provisions.content: (1) Any person operating livestock production unit shall avail the following for inspection: (a) ownership of land; (b) map and land use plan; (c) feeding plan; (d) water management plan; (e) herd health program; (f) herd breeding program; (g) identification record system; (h) pasture management plan; (i) off take plan; (j) replacement plan; and (k) annual activity plan. (2) A person producing livestock in traditional way shall avail the following for inspection: (a) right to use communal grazing land ; (b) off take plan; (c) disease control mechanism; and (d) identification record system. (3) An Inspector shall inspect all livestock production units for compliance set out in the Fourth Schedule of these Regulations.

provisions.title: Animal welfare requirements
provisions.content: Any person operating livestock production unit, for purpose of these Regulations; shall meet the following animal welfare requirements: (a) provide adequate space for animal feed and water troughs; and (b) provide appropriate animal health and handling facilities free from fear, stress, pain or inability to express natural behavior.

provisions.title: Inspection of livestock market
provisions.content: (1) An Inspector shall inspect any primary, secondary and border livestock market to ascertain the compliance with conditions as prescribed in these Regulations. (2) Notwithstanding sub regulation (1) , the inspection shall base on the checklist set out in the Fifth Schedule.

provisions.title: Slaughter facilities inspection requirement
provisions.content: (1) Any person owning or operating a slaughter facility shall— (a) avail to standard sanitary operating procedures (SSOPS) as set out in the Sixth Schedule; (b) follow an order of from dirty to clean areas or production; (c) have lair age, sink pen, condemnation pit and sewage tanks; (d) use appropriate working equipment and tools of food standard; and (e) have clean and safe amount of recommended 200 liters of water per cattle in meat production process. (2) A person shall not operate slaughter facility without complying to humane slaughter, standard sanitary operating procedures ( SSOPs ) and HACCP.

provisions.title: Butchery shops inspection requirement
provisions.content: Any person owning or operating a butchery shall avail to standard operating procedures set out in the Seventh Schedule: (a) in meat cutting process follow an order of from dirty to clean areas; (b) separate different tasks in the butchery; (c) use of appropriate working equipment and tools of stainless steel food standard; (d) have washing sinks, clean and safe water in the process of meat butchering; and (e) observe general cleanness to working tools.

provisions.title: Environment requirement
provisions.content: The owner or agent of the establishment shall meet measures that reduce methane emissions or environmental pollution impact including: (a) collection, treatment and disposal of liquid wastes; (b) collection, treatment and disposal of solid wastes; (c) protection of aquatic and terrestrial ecosystems; (d) storage of medications and disposal of empty containers, disposable and defective treatment tools and storage of expired drugs and chemicals; and (e) handling and disposal of condemned carcass, organs or dead animals. Part IV – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: (1) A person who— (a) delays or obstructs any inspector in the exercise of his powers under these Regulations; (b) refuses or fails without reasonable cause to give any information which he is lawfully required to give under these Regulations; or (c) gives any information, which is false in a material particular or which he reasonably knows to be untrue; commits an offence and shall upon conviction be liable to a fine of not less than fifty thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding twelve months or both. (2) Any person who contravenes any provisions of these Regulations commits an offence and upon conviction shall be liable to a fine of not less than fifty thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding four months or both.
